2606.07040.title: Beyond Rubrics: Exploration-Guided Evaluation Skills for Reward Modeling
2606.07040.paper_summary: Open-ended reward modeling requires judges that can follow subtle, domain-specific preferences when verifiable answers are unavailable. Existing rubric-based methods often address this by generating criteria online for each query, but the extra generation step can add inference overhead and produce rigid or misaligned guidance. We introduce Eval-Skill, an exploration-guided method that synthesizes reusable evaluation skills for reward modeling and reframes reward guidance as context evolution rather than parameter training or per-query rubric generation. Using only 100 cases per domain for skill evolution, Eval-Skill synthesizes reusable domain-level evaluation skills through two progressive stages, workflow generation followed by principle generation, with exploration and selection interleaved across both stages. Once generated, a skill is directly injected into the judge context. Across multiple RM benchmarks, Eval-Skill consistently improves diverse judge backbones; on RewardBench 2, it yields significant gains over vanilla judging for each main backbone (+13.44% for Qwen3-8B, and 18.51% for DeepSeek-V4-Flash). Further analyses of evolution-time scaling, generalizability, and transferability show that compact evaluation skills offer an efficient new paradigm for LLM-based evaluation. Code is available at https://github.com/xing-stellus-yue/Eval-Skill.
2606.07040.paper_summary_zh: 開放式獎勵建模需要能夠在可驗證答案不可用時，遵循微妙的特定領域偏好的評審。現有的基於評分標準的方法通常通過為每個查詢在線生成標準來解決這一問題，但額外的生成步驟可能會增加推理開銷，並產生僵化或不對齊的指導。我們介紹了 Eval-Skill，一種探索引導的方法，合成可重用的評估技能以進行獎勵建模，並將獎勵指導重新框架為上下文演變，而不是參數訓練或每查詢的評分標準生成。僅使用每個領域 100 個案例進行技能演變，Eval-Skill 通過兩個漸進階段合成可重用的領域級評估技能，首先是工作流程生成，然後是原則生成，並在兩個階段之間交錯進行探索和選擇。一旦生成，技能會直接注入到評審上下文中。在多個 RM 基準測試中，Eval-Skill 一直在改善多樣化的評審骨幹；在 RewardBench 2 上，它為每個主要骨幹帶來了顯著的增益（Qwen3-8B 增加 13.44%，DeepSeek-V4-Flash 增加 18.51%）。對演變時間縮放、可泛化性和可轉移性的進一步分析顯示，緊湊的評估技能為基於 LLM 的評估提供了一種高效的新範式。代碼可在 https://github.com/xing-stellus-yue/Eval-Skill 獲得。
2606.07040.author: Xing Yue et.al.
2606.07040.authors: Xing Yue, Linjuan Wu, Daoxin Zhang, Yongliang Shen, Weiming Lu
